0.q: the salt tolerance and the expression levels of these two transcription factors could be significantly upregulated when treated with exogenous application of ACC, while reduced when exposure to AgNO3, suggesting that RsCBF2 and RsERF18 positively regulated the salt tolerance in a manner of dependent on ethylene synthesis pathway.

1.q: the salt tolerance and the expression levels of these two transcription factors could be significantly upregulated when treated with exogenous application of ACC, while reduced when exposure to AgNO3, suggesting that RsCBF2 and RsERF18 positively regulated the salt tolerance in a manner of dependent on ethylene synthesis pathway.

2.q: Y1H, dual-luciferase assay (DLA) and electrophoretic mobility shift assays (EMSA) showed that these two genes could active the transcription of RsPIP2-1 by directly binding to the DRE/CRT element and GCC-box element in its promoter.
